codeReview.summary: Comprehensive implementation of webhook reliability features covering all three validation contract assertions (VAL-REL-011, VAL-REL-012, VAL-REL-013). The implementation includes HMAC-SHA256 signatures with per-share secrets, strict URL validation rejecting private/internal addresses, and retry logic with proper status classification. Code quality is high with proper TypeScript typing, comprehensive test coverage (31 new tests), and all 132 tests passing.

issues.description: Webhook delivery uses fire-and-forget pattern with .catch() but the ExecutionContext (ctx) is not passed to handlers, so ctx.waitUntil() cannot be used. The retry logic is implemented correctly but may not complete if the Worker shuts down before the 5-second retry delay. Consider updating the fetch handler signature to pass ctx and use ctx.waitUntil() for guaranteed delivery.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The AGENTS.md and worker patterns should document how to handle background tasks that need guaranteed execution. The current fetch handler signature (request, env) doesn't include ExecutionContext, preventing use of ctx.waitUntil(). The spike handler works around this with fire-and-forget but this doesn't guarantee webhook delivery during Worker shutdown.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker/src/index.ts:17 - fetch handler signature is async fetch(request: Request, env: Env). worker/src/handlers/spikes.ts:160-176 - createWebhookDeliveryTask called with .catch() but no waitUntil wrapper.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker-backend skill was followed correctly. The implementation includes proper test coverage (31 tests), database migrations (005_webhook_secret.sql), and follows the modular architecture pattern established in the foundation milestone.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows followedProcedure: true. Migration file created at worker/migrations/005_webhook_secret.sql. Tests cover all three validation assertions with comprehensive edge cases.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation correctly uses the Web Crypto API (crypto.subtle) for HMAC-SHA256 operations and implements constant-time comparison for signature verification to prevent timing attacks. This is a security best practice that should be documented for future crypto implementations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker/src/utils/crypto.ts:215-268 - computeWebhookSignature and verifyWebhookSignature use crypto.subtle with HMAC-SHA256 and constantTimeEqual for signature comparison.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No new services or commands needed to be added to services.yaml. The webhook feature integrates with the existing wrangler-dev service and uses standard fetch API.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: No changes needed to .factory/services.yaml - webhook delivery is an internal background operation triggered by spike creation.

summary: The webhook-reliability feature is fully implemented and passes review. All three validation contract requirements are met: HMAC-SHA256 signatures with per-share secrets in t=timestamp,v1=hex_hmac format (VAL-REL-011), HTTPS URL validation rejecting private IPs, localhost, and internal domains (VAL-REL-012), and retry logic with 5-second delay and proper status classification (VAL-REL-013). The implementation includes 31 comprehensive tests, proper TypeScript typing, migration support, and follows security best practices including constant-time signature comparison and cryptographically random secrets. All 132 tests pass and typecheck succeeds. One non-blocking issue noted: the fetch handler doesn't pass ExecutionContext to handlers, so ctx.waitUntil() cannot be used for guaranteed background task completion.
